data.title: Interactive Brokers allows clients to fund accounts using stablecoins
data.description:
Interactive Brokers (IBKR) is enabling eligible global clients to fund their brokerage accounts using stablecoins, it said on Thursday,

The new capability, introduced last month, offers near-instant processing and around-the-clock availability, allowing clients to deposit funds and start trading across 170 global markets immediately. 

Clients can fund their accounts by sending dollar-pegged stablecoin USDC (USDC-USD) from their crypto wallet to a secure wallet provided via a partnership with zerohash. 

Unlike traditional wire transfers, “stablecoin funding provides international investors with the speed and flexibility required in today’s markets. Clients can transfer funds and begin trading within minutes, while also reducing transaction costs,” said Interactive Brokers CEO Milan Galik said in a statement.

The company said it also plans to add support next week for Ripple Coin (RLUSD-USD) and PayPal's coin (PYUSD-USD).

Interactive Brokers (IBKR) noted it does not charge fees for stablecoin deposits, while zerohash applies a conversion fee of 0.30% per deposit.

IBKR shares climbed 5.3% in late morning trading.

data.title: Rails rumpus: Union Pacific defends the potential benefits of a merger with Norfolk Southern
data.description:
Union Pacific Corporation (UNP) continues to defend the company's plans to merge with Norfolk Southern (NSC). 

The rails company met with customers at the winter meeting of the Midwest Association of Rail Shippers to discuss the benefits of the proposed merger and respond to misinformation from opponents. Union Pacific (UNP) said the meeting provided a welcome opportunity to correct misinformation spread by opponents.

"We knew our competitors would oppose the merger, and we understand why," stated Union Pacific (UNP) CEO Jim Vena. "This is a transformational merger that will inject more competition into the railroad industry and force them to enhance their service, reduce their price, or do both," he added.

Last month, Union Pacific (UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NSC) submitted their application to the Surface Transportation Board seeking approval to create America’s first transcontinental railroad. The filing, which includes extensive analysis from independent experts, was noted to demonstrate in detail how a seamless coast-to-coast railroad will provide faster service, greater reliability and a lower cost structure. Union Pacific (UNO) said the application included a record-setting 2,000 letters of support from customers, public officials, industry associations and unions, demonstrating broad support.

The combined rails company will also be more resilient due to the greater availability of main line track, terminals, crews, locomotives, and rail cars required to keep traffic flowing, according to UNP> A transcontinental network with 50,000 route miles is also anticipated to have more options for rapidly rerouting traffic to avoid congested areas or weather.

Earlier in the week, Canadian National Railway Company (CNI) announced  that it has filed a motion with the Surface Transportation Board to compel additional information in relation to the rail merger plans of Union Pacific (UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NSC). The company said its motion shows that the applicants have not been upfront with their assessment and failed to outline the full extent of competitive harms as a result of the merger.

data.title: U.S. natural gas inventory net change of -71 Bcf for week ended January 9 - EIA
data.description:
• Stocks for the week ended January 9 were  3,185 Bcf vs. 3,256 Bcf for the week ended January 2.
  • Net change: -71 Bcf vs. -119 Bcf for the week ended January 2. 
  • Consensus: -89B.
  • ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (FCG), (UNL), (HNU:CA)
  • Click here to read the full EIA Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report.

data.title: Altai Capital nominates two directors to OraSure's board
data.description:
• Altai Capital nominated two candidates for election to OraSure's (OSUR) board. Orasure rose 1.7%.
  • Altai Capital, which has a 5.2% stake in OraSure (OSUR), nominated Digital Diagnostics CEO John Bertrand and Altai President Rishi Bajaj to the board, according to a statement on Thursday.
  • The nominations come after Altai's letter last month to the board outlining its concerns about the company’s "significant share price underperformance" and the board’s refusal to consider alternative strategies.

data.title: GE Healthcare drops as UBS cuts to Sell on valuation
data.description:
GE HealthCare (GEHC) traded lower on Thursday after UBS downgraded GE’s (GE) healthcare spinoff to Sell from Neutral, arguing that the company at its current valuation doesn’t adequately reflect its risks.

Analyst Graham Doyle noted that the Chicago, Illinois-based medical equipment maker, trading less than 10% below its all-time high, is significantly outperforming its peers due to investor enthusiasm over its tariff mitigation efforts and new products such as its Flyrcado diagnostic agent.

“Whilst these are clear positives, we feel the shares and consensus capture their best possible execution,” Doyle wrote, adding that UBS has set EBIT estimates for the company at 0%-7% below consensus, assuming “more gradual product ramps.”

The analyst cited additional competitive headwinds that could further hurt his estimates, including increased rivalry from Chinese players and the recent launch of a U.S. competitor targeting the contrast agent Omnipaque, which accounts for 9% of GEHC’s EBIT.

While the company can meet expectations for Q4 and the 2026 outlook, “we think delivering 2026 will be a challenge, while the Flyrcado launch, China competition, and Omnipaque competition risks could materialize in H2,” Doyle wrote. The analyst indicated a 12-month price target of $77 for GEHC compared to $73 previously.

data.title: Paramount fails to get its suit against Warner Bros. expedited: report
data.description:
A Delaware judge refused to expedite Paramount Skydance Corp.’s (PSKY) lawsuit that accused directors of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. (WBD) of misleading investors about a more than $82.7B buyout bid from Netflix Inc. (NFLX), Bloomberg reported on Thursday.

According to the report, Judge Morgan Zurn found that Paramount’s demands didn’t meet legal tests for fast-tracking a hearing into accusations that Warner Bros.’ board made erroneous disclosures about the deal.

Developing story…

data.title: Wells Fargo upgrades Broadcom, Analog Devices and Monolithic Power Systems to Overweight
data.description:
Wells Fargo upgraded Broadcom (AVGO), Analog Devices (ADI) and Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) to Overweight from Equal-weight.

Shares of these three were up 2.6%, 1.5% and 4%, respectively, during early market action on Thursday.

The financial firm edged up Broadcom's price target to $430 from $410.

"We think the recent pull-back in shares of Broadcom, coupled with increasing confidence in potentially meaningful incremental catalysts looking through 2026, gives us the opportunity to get constructive," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Aaron Rakers, in an in-depth investor report on Thursday.

They highlighted Broadcom's key partnership with Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) in developing its tensor processing units. 

"In addition to Broadcom's announcement that Anthropic (ANTHRO) has ordered $21B in Google TPUs + rack-scale systems, we think indications of potential Google TPU deployments with Meta (META), xAI (X.AI), Apple (AAPL), SSI, and others would be potentially meaningful incremental upside catalysts," Rakers noted.

Meanwhile, Wells Fargo hiked its price target on Analog Devices to $340 from $265.

"With ADI continuing to report positive bookings activity across key growth end markets and signs of a broad market recovery, we expect the company to deliver modest upside to consensus estimates that currently reflect relatively seasonal growth throughout F2026," Rakers said.

Analog Devices reported 40% year-over-year growth during 2025 in its automated test equipment segment. Momentum is expected to continue in 2026 due to leading edge logic and high-bandwidth memory demand.

Finally, the bank inched up Monolithic Power's price target to $1,125 from $1,100.

"Following a reset year in 2025 as NVIDIA (NVDA) expanded its supplier ecosystem, we expect MPWR to deliver accelerating  growth in its Enterprise data segment," Rakers noted. "While we model revenue +39% y/y in 2026, we will be focused on upside drivers as demand visibility improves throughout the year."

data.title: Digital Realty Trust raised to Buy at HSBC on robust 2026 demand outlook
data.description:
Digital Realty Trust (DLR) was raised to Buy from Hold at HSBC Global Investment Research on the back of a robust 2026 demand outlook.

The price target was raised to $193 from $187. Shares were 1.68% higher during Thursday morning trading to $161.94.

The research firm believes that power constraints are still a key factor for the traditional data centers, and oversupply concerns are likely premature for now.

For DLR, the research note said, "We think market concerns on 2027 growth look overdone. We expect 10% revenue and 9% AFFO per share CAGRs 2025e-28e for DLR. Our higher TPs (target price) are driven by rolling forward our valuation base to 2027e (from 2026e), partly offset by lower target multiples."

HSBC's rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors ratings of Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system grades the stock as Hold.

data.title: Citi upgrades Intel, removes Micron from Focus List
data.description:
Investment firm Citi upgraded Intel (INTC) and removed Micron (MU) from its U.S. Focus List on Thursday.

Intel

Intel was raised to Neutral from Sell and put a $50 price target, as the firm believes it should benefit from advanced packaging tightness at Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM). In addition, it has a “unique window of opportunity to attract foundry wafer customers with the support of the US government,” analyst Atif Malik said.

“We believe Intel will get AI ASIC spillover demand e.g. TPUs in the back end first and then front-end foundry customers as process yields on 18A-P/14A improve,” Malik added.

Despite the aforementioned positives — as well as the U.S. government being an investor and the prospects of “stabilizing” capex — Intel is not completely out of the woods, Malik added. It is also expected to lose CPU market share to AMD (AMD) and Arm (ARM), and there is the chance that the PC market softens, giving rising memory prices, the analyst posited.

Micron

Micron was removed from the U.S. Focus List, as Malik said the pricing momentum for dynamic random access memory likely “decelerates” in the second-quarter from the first quarter. 

“Micron stock typically follows the [quarter-over-quarter] pricing momentum,” Malik explained. “That said, we expect extended memory [supply-demand] balance through 2026/27 on limited fab space availability and strong AI demand.”

data.title: Tamarack Valley Energy renews normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Tamarack Valley Energy (TVE:CA) said on Thursday  that the TSX approved its application to renew its normal course issuer bid (NCIB).
  • NCIB would allow the company to repurchase up to 47.7M shares, representing 10% of public float, over 12 months from Jan. 19, 2026. The NCIB will expire by January 18, 2027.
  • The company may repurchase up to 0.41M shares daily, equal to 25% of the average daily trading volume on the TSX over the six months ended Dec. 31, 2025.
  • Under its expiring NCIB, the company was allowed to repurchase up to 51.28M shares between Jan. 19, 2025, and Jan. 18, 2026, and had bought back 34.42M shares at an average price of C$5.01 per share as of Dec. 31, 2025.
